entries.definition:
-ى
suckle, (of a child) to seek breast-feeding (2:233) وَإِنْ أَرَدْتُمْ أَنْ
تَسْتَرْضِعُوا أَوْلَادَكُمْ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذَا سَلَّمْتُمْ مَا آتَيْتُمْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ nor will there
be any blame if you seek to breast-feed [engage a wet nurse for]
your children, provided you pay as agreed, in a fair manner.

رَضَاعَة raḍāʿatun [v. n.; n.] breast-feeding, suckling (4:23)
حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهَاتُكُمْ ... وَأَخَوَاتُكُمْ مِنَ الرَّضَاعَةِ ... forbidden to you are
your mothers ... and your sisters [by breast-feeding].

مُرْضِعَة murḍiʿatun [act. part. fem.] one who is breast-feeding or
nursing a baby (22:2) يَوْمَ تَرَوْنَهَا تَذْهَلُ كُلُّ مُرْضِعَةٍ عَمَّا أَرْضَعَتْ on the
Day you see it, every suckling/nursing woman will be oblivious to
whom she suckles/nurses.

مَرَاضِع marāḍiʿ [pl. of fem. n. مُرْضِع murḍiʿ; pl. of v. n./n. of
place مَرْضَع marḍaʿ] breast-feeders, foster mothers; (act of)
feeding through breasts/teats (28:12) وَحَرَّمْنَا عَلَيْهِ الْمَرَاضِعَ مِنْ قَبْلُ and
We had previously made wet nurses unacceptable to him.

-ى

We had previously made wet nurses unacceptable to him.

entries.definition:
-ى r–ḍ–w/y to accept, to agree, to consent, to be pleased, to be
satisfied, approval, contentment, acceptance; favour, grace. Of
this root, 11 forms occur 73 times in the Qur’an: رَضِيَ raḍiya 38
times; يُرْضِي yurḍī three times; تَرَاضَى tarāḍā twice; اِرْتَضَى ʾirtaḍā
three times; تَرَاضٍ tarāḍin twice; رَاضِيَة rāḍiyatun four times; رَضِيّ
raḍiyy once; رِضْوَان riḍwān 13 times; مَرْضِيّ mardiyy once;
مَرْضِيَّة mardiyyatun once and مَرْضَات marḍāt five times.

رَضِيَ raḍiya a [v. intrans.] 1 to attain inner peace, to attain
contentment (20:130) وَمِنْ آنَاءِ اللَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْ وَأَطْرَافَ النَّهَارِ لَعَلَّكَ تَرْضَى and
at the beginning and end of the day celebrate His praise that you
may attain contentment 2 to accept [without condition] (5:119)
رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ God accepts them and they accept Him 3 to
be satisfied with, to be content with (10:7) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَاءَنَا
وَرَضُوا بِالْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا those who are not concerned to meet Us and are
satisfied with the life of this world 4 to approve of (39:7) وَلَا يَرْضَى
لِعِبَادِهِ الْكُفْرَ and He does not approve of ingratitude/disbelief in His
servants 5 to forgive, pardon (9:96) يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ لِتَرْضَوْا عَنْهُمْ فَإِنْ تَرْضَوْا
عَنْهُمْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَرْضَى عَنِ الْقَوْمِ الْفَاسِقِينَ they will swear to you in order to
make you forgive them, but even if you do so, God will not forgive

369
entries.definition_english:
-ى r–ḍ–w/y to accept, to agree, to consent, to be pleased, to be
satisfied, approval, contentment, acceptance; favour, grace. Of
this root, 11 forms occur 73 times in the Qur’an: رَضِيَ raḍiya 38

three times; تَرَاضٍ tarāḍin twice; رَاضِيَة rāḍiyatun four times; رَضِيّ
raḍiyy once; رِضْوَان riḍwān 13 times; مَرْضِيّ mardiyy once;
مَرْضِيَّة mardiyyatun once and مَرْضَات marḍāt five times.

رَضِيَ raḍiya a [v. intrans.] 1 to attain inner peace, to attain
contentment (20:130) وَمِنْ آنَاءِ اللَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْ وَأَطْرَافَ النَّهَارِ لَعَلَّكَ تَرْضَى and
at the beginning and end of the day celebrate His praise that you
may attain contentment 2 to accept [without condition] (5:119)
رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ God accepts them and they accept Him 3 to
be satisfied with, to be content with (10:7) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَاءَنَا
وَرَضُوا بِالْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا those who are not concerned to meet Us and are
satisfied with the life of this world 4 to approve of (39:7) وَلَا يَرْضَى
لِعِبَادِهِ الْكُفْرَ and He does not approve of ingratitude/disbelief in His
servants 5 to forgive, pardon (9:96) يَحْلِفُونَ لَكُمْ لِتَرْضَوْا عَنْهُمْ فَإِنْ تَرْضَوْا
عَنْهُمْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَرْضَى عَنِ الْقَوْمِ الْفَاسِقِينَ they will swear to you in order to
make you forgive them, but even if you do so, God will not forgive

369

entries.definition:
-ى
those who rebel [against Him].

يُرْضِي yurḍī [imperf. of v. IV أَرْضَى ʾarḍā, trans.] to satisfy, to
seek approval, to flatter (9:8) يُرْضُونَكُمْ بِأَفْوَاهِهِمْ وَتَأْبَى قُلُوبُهُمْ they give
you satisfaction with their mouths, but their hearts refuse.

تَرَاضَى tarāḍā [v. VI, intrans.] to come to a mutual agreement
(2:232) فَلَا تَعْضُلُوهُنَّ أَنْ يَنْكِحْنَ أَزْوَاجَهُنَّ إِذَا تَرَاضَوْا بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ do not
prevent them from remarrying their husbands if they agree
mutually to do so in a fair manner.

اِرْتَضَى ʾirtaḍā [v. VIII, trans.] to accept as fitting, to approve of
something as suitable, to select, to choose (24:55) وَلَيُمَكِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ
دِينَهُمُ الَّذِي ارْتَضَى لَهُمْ and He will surely establish for them their religion
which He had approved for them.

تَرَاضٍ tarāḍin [v. n.] mutual agreement (2:233) فَإِنْ أَرَادَا فِصَالًا عَنْ
تَرَاضٍ مِنْهُمَا وَتَشَاوُرٍ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا but if, by mutual consent and
consultation, the couple wish to wean [the child], they will not be
blamed.

رَاضِيَة rāḍiyatun [act. part. fem.] 1 approving, liking, content,
satisfied, well-pleased (89:28) ارْجِعِي إِلَى رَبِّكِ رَاضِيَةً مَرْضِيَّةً return to
your Lord, content and well approved of 2 [in the sense of the pass.
part] approved of, accepted (69:21) فَهُوَ فِي عِيشَةٍ رَاضِيَةٍ and so he
will be in agreeable [lit. approved of] life.

رَضِيّ raḍiyy [quasi-pass. part.] agreeable, acceptable, being
approved of (19:6) يَرِثُنِي وَيَرِثُ مِنْ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ وَاجْعَلْهُ رَبِّ رَضِيًّا who shall
inherit me and inherit the family of Jacob; Lord, and make him
acceptable [to You].

رِضْوَان riḍwān [v. n./n.] 1 God’s favour, pleasure, favour (9:72)
وَرِضْوَانٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ ذَلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ and, greater still, acceptance
from God, that is the supreme triumph 2 God’s acceptance
(9:109) أَفَمَنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيَانَهُ عَلَى تَقْوَى مِنَ اللَّهِ وَرِضْوَانٍ is he who founds
his building on consciousness of God and desire for His
acceptance better?

مَرْضِيّ mardiyy [pass. part., fem. مَرْضِيَّة mardiyyatun]
acceptable, well-received (19:55) وَكَانَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِ مَرْضِيًّا and he was
well-received by his Lord.

مَرْضَات marḍāt [n.] 1 pleasure (2:207) وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَشْرِى نَفْسَهُ ٱبْتِغَآءَ مَرْضَاتِ ٱللَّهِ but there is also a kind of person who gives his life away seeking the pleasure of God 2 favour, acceptance, approval (66:1) يَٰأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ مَا أَحَلَّ ٱللَّهُ لَكَ تَبْتَغِى مَرْضَاتَ أَزْوَٰجِكَ Prophet, why do you prohibit what God has made lawful to you—seeking your wives’ approval?

-ى
those who rebel [against Him].

(9:109) أَفَمَنْ أَسَّسَ بُنْيَانَهُ عَلَى تَقْوَى مِنَ اللَّهِ وَرِضْوَانٍ is he who founds
his building on consciousness of God and desire for His
acceptance better?

مَرْضِيّ mardiyy [pass. part., fem. مَرْضِيَّة mardiyyatun]
acceptable, well-received (19:55) وَكَانَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِ مَرْضِيًّا and he was
well-received by his Lord.

مَرْضَات marḍāt [n.] 1 pleasure (2:207) وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يَشْرِى نَفْسَهُ ٱبْتِغَآءَ مَرْضَاتِ ٱللَّهِ but there is also a kind of person who gives his life away seeking the pleasure of God 2 favour, acceptance, approval (66:1) يَٰأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ مَا أَحَلَّ ٱللَّهُ لَكَ تَبْتَغِى مَرْضَاتَ أَزْوَٰجِكَ Prophet, why do you prohibit what God has made lawful to you—seeking your wives’ approval?
